summary: Screw feed technology has no established role in medical waste handling; its claimed effectiveness for managing pathological waste contradicts strict regulations requiring prior decontamination through methods like autoclaving or incineration. Therefore, stating it is “ideal” for pathological waste lacks both evidentiary basis and compliance with biosafety guidelines.
